channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Carpet dumped on pavement, 24th July
item.description:
Hi yesterday I reported carpet dumped on pavement outside 18 Stoke Newington Common. Today you said it's been fixed but all you have done is put the carpet into the garden of number 18 (my house) not acceptable. Please clear properly

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Fountayne Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubber mat perished in park, 24th July
item.description:
Rubber matting on playpark perished causing trip hazards

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): St John Fisher Road, West Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Loads of bikes suddenly on my street, 24th July
item.description:
I don’t know why but suddenly there were about 20 Voi bikes on my street and they’re just on the floor dumped

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 245, Queen's Road, Nunhead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish/farces/broken glass all over pavement, 24th July
item.description:
Someone dumped a bag of rubbish which inevitably foxes have ripped open as they search for food, but there is faeces and broken glass and it’s right in the middle of the pavement

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Buchan Road, Nunhead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: A Fly-tip Large - More than 10 black bags problem has been found, 24th July
item.description:
A Fly-tip Large - More than 10 black bags problem has been found by Brent Council

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Alpha Place, Kilburn, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Outside 16, 24th July
item.description:
Dead tree

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 15, Inglewood Road, West Hampstead, Greater London

item.title: Dangerous bag dumped, 24th July
item.description:
It looks potentially hazardous waste dumped on a residential street

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 40, Burleigh Gardens, Brunswick Park, Greater London

item.title: Sprayed tp, 24th July
item.description:
TP sprayed need to be cleaned

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Longhope Close, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree needs watering, 24th July
item.description:
This tree is in need of water. It is fitted with a watering bag but it is wilting and it looks as though it has not been watered this year. Please put it back on the list for watering as I live too far away to carry water to it! Thank you

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Black Horse Court, Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 29 Brondesbury villas, 24th July
item.description:
See photo

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Brondesbury Villas, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned Line bikes AGAIN left on roadway yellow line, blocking driveway access (also on bus route), 24th July
item.description:
4 bikes - these need to be moved immediately as blocking the road and access. Also hirers need to be fined and banded from hiring in future. If this continues to occur, Camden should stop using this area for bike storage, as too small for the number of bikes and also was blocking both driveways and either side. Area not suitable and should be relocated

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 96, Canfield Gardens, South Hampstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping, 24th July
item.description:
Rubbish everywhere including large items

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Dutton Street, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Been here since before 9th June, 24th July
item.description:
Someone needs to come with a shovel to scrape this off the street - photos showing it there over a month ago. Road sweepers need to come down here regularly.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Madron Street, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: St George flags attached to street lights, 24th July
item.description:
Contrary to the Highways Act 1980, flags have been attached to the lampposts and need to be removed.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Park Mews, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown weeds along the pavement and kerb, 24th July
item.description:
Weeds and other vegetation have become overgrown along the pavement, kerb and around the drainage areas. They are making the street look neglected, narrowing parts of the footway and trapping litter and debris. It seems to be encouraging dog owners to leave their dog mess in and around the weeds. Please arrange for the weeds to be removed, including growth from the kerb edges, paving joints and around the drains, and for the area to be swept and cleared afterwards.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 11, Shawbury Road, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown trees, 24th July
item.description:
There are lots of overgrown trees and thorny branches overhanging into the pavement and spilling onto the road almost. It is hazardous for pedestrians, cyclists and drivers.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Lower Hall Lane, Edmonton
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Tree overhanging the highway from private property

item.title: Ongoing Concern – Litter Bin Not Being Checked at Weekends, 24th July
item.description:
I am writing to raise an urgent concern regarding waste management in the alleyway adjacent to 79 King Hall Road, leading towards Clock House Station. The refuse bin in this area is frequently overflowing, resulting in significant litter buildup and attracting vermin such as rats and foxes. This situation presents serious health and hygiene risks to both residents and pedestrians, including potential bacterial spread, unpleasant odours, and an increased risk of slips or injuries. It appears that this location may be consistently missed during routine waste collections. To help address these concerns and maintain acceptable hygiene standards, I would strongly recommend increasing collection frequency to at least four times per week. In addition, the litter bin needs to be checked over the weekend (Saturday or Sunday) to prevent further overflow and ensure the area remains clean. I kindly urge the relevant team to investigate this matter as a priority and implement a sustainable solution to ensure regular maintenance and cleanliness of the area. Thank you for your attention to this matter.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Kings Hall Road, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bin bags dumped on corner of lordship lane and shawbury, 24th July
item.description:
Several refuse bags and loose waste have been dumped on the pavement at the corner of Lordship Lane and Shawbury Road. The rubbish is obstructing part of the pedestrian crossing area and tactile paving, creating difficulties for pedestrians, particularly wheelchair users and people with visual impairments. Some of the bags are already open and food waste has been scattered along the pavement and kerb on Shawbury road which is attracting pests and making the area unhygienic. Please arrange for the bags and all associated loose waste to be removed and the affected area cleaned.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 2, Shawbury Road, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overflowing bin, 24th July
item.description:
Hi there, this bin is overflowing again. People that stop in the loading area are emptying their cars of rubbish daily, so it’s repeatedly overflowing. This is the third time I’ve reported this week. I think this bin should be removed because it is a continual problem - either that or it needs to be emptied daily. The pavement is also becoming sloppy from all the food waste being pulled out of the bin by foxes.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 74, Whateley Road, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
